name: task-runner
description: Run project commands with just. Check for justfile in project root, list available tasks, execute common operations like test, build, lint. Triggers on: run tests, build project, list tasks, check available commands, run script, project commands.
capabilities: List all available recipes in a project justfile
Run specific recipes like test, build, and lint
Check for justfile existence in project root
author: 0xDarkMatter
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill with no executable code. Contains only SKILL.md (documentation for just CLI tool) and skill-report.json (metadata report). All static findings are false positives caused by markdown formatting and JSON metadata being misidentified as security patterns.
valueStatement: Developers waste time searching for build instructions and memorizing project-specific commands. This skill provides a standardized way to discover and run any task defined in a project justfile, from testing to deployment.
limitations: Requires just CLI tool to be installed separately
Cannot run commands outside of defined justfile recipes
Limited to recipes defined in project justfile

useCases.title: Streamline Development Workflow
useCases.description: Quickly discover and run project commands without reading documentation.

useCases.title: Standardize Build Processes
useCases.description: Execute consistent build, test, and deployment commands across projects.

useCases.title: Onboard to Unknown Projects
useCases.description: Explore unfamiliar codebases by listing available commands.

promptTemplates.title: List Available Tasks
promptTemplates.prompt: What commands are available in this project? Show me all the just recipes.

promptTemplates.title: Run Tests
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the tests for this project using just.

promptTemplates.title: Build Project
promptTemplates.prompt: Build this project. What build commands are available?

promptTemplates.title: Deploy with Args
promptTemplates.prompt: Deploy to production using just. The deploy command takes an environment argument.

bestPractices: Check for a justfile immediately when entering a new project using 'just --list'
Use 'just --show <recipe>' to understand what a command does before running it
Run recipes with arguments by providing them after the recipe name
antiPatterns: Running unknown just recipes without checking what they do first
Assuming just is installed without verifying
Hardcoding commands that should be defined in the justfile

faq.question: How do I install just?
faq.answer: Install with brew install just on macOS or cargo install just on Linux, Windows, and macOS.

faq.question: What if no justfile exists?
faq.answer: The skill will report that no justfile was found. Ask the project maintainers to add one.

faq.question: Can I run commands from subdirectories?
faq.answer: Yes, use just --justfile path/to/justfile <recipe> to run recipes from subdirectory justfiles.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only reads justfile configurations and runs predefined recipes. It does not access or modify other files.

faq.question: Why is my command not working?
faq.answer: Ensure just is installed. Verify the recipe name matches exactly. Check that a justfile exists in the project.

faq.question: How is this different from make?
faq.answer: Just is simpler, cross-platform, and uses shell syntax. It does not require tabs and has more intuitive recipe definitions.
